disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Progressive symmetrical numbness and tingling of both feet starting in the toes and ascending over about a month, in a patient who has recently completed chemotherapy
trigger_question_examples: Tell me about the numbness — where did it start and how has it changed?
Have you had any recent treatments like chemotherapy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started as pins and needles in my toes about a month ago, right after my chemo, and it's slowly crept up both feet the same on each side.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden severe burning, shooting pain ascending both legs tonight, completely unresponsive to regular paracetamol
trigger_question_examples: What is the pain like and what have you tried for it?
Is the paracetamol helping at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Tonight it turned into a burning, shooting pain that shot straight up both legs, and the paracetamol I take four times a day does nothing for it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Mechanical fall due to unsteadiness in the dark from loss of sensation in the feet, now unable to get up off the floor and unwilling to weight-bear
trigger_question_examples: How did the fall happen and can you get up now?
Are you able to stand or walk at the moment?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I couldn't feel the floor and lost my balance in the dark and crashed into the wall. I'm still down on the floor and I can't get myself up — I'm too scared to put weight on my legs.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No head strike and no loss of consciousness with the fall
trigger_question_examples: Did you hit your head or lose consciousness?
Did you black out at any point?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I didn't hit my head and I didn't black out — I just went down against the wall.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No weakness or paralysis of the legs, and no loss of bladder or bowel control (pertinent negatives for cord compression)
trigger_question_examples: Can you still move your legs and feet?
Have you had any loss of control of your bladder or bowels, or numbness around the saddle area?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can still move my legs, they're just painful and I can't feel much. I haven't lost control of my bladder or bowels.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new focal back pain or midline spinal tenderness after the fall
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any new pain in your back or spine?
Is there a particular painful spot along your backbone?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my back doesn't hurt — it's my feet and legs, the burning nerve pain, not my spine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone with no one available to assist, dim hallway lighting and loose rugs — high ongoing falls and unnoticed-injury risk
trigger_question_examples: Is there anyone at home with you tonight?
Is there anything around the house that makes falling more likely?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own, there's no one here. The hallway light is dim and I've got a few loose rugs down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Extreme cold hypersensitivity of the feet
trigger_question_examples: Does anything in particular set the foot symptoms off?
How do your feet feel against the cold?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The cold floor makes my feet feel terrible — they're really oversensitive to cold.
